scheme_name: Haryana Merit Cum Means Scheme For Minority
page_title: Haryana Merit Cum Means Scheme For Minority

details: Launched in 2012, the scheme "Merit Cum Means Based Scholarship" has been implemented by the Ministry of Minority Affairs, Government of India, and aims to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from minority communities to pursue professional and technical courses. Through this scheme, monetary benefits are provided to support education-related expenses for eligible students belonging to minority groups, ensuring their socio-economic empowerment. The scheme is implemented by the State Government of Haryana in collaboration with the Union Ministry of Minority Affairs. The primary objectives of the scheme are to encourage higher education, support meritorious students from economically weaker backgrounds, and promote equitable education opportunities for minority communities.
benefits: For Hostellers: ₹30,000/- per annum, including ₹10,000/- as maintenance allowance and up to ₹20,000/- as course fees.
For Day Scholars: ₹25,000/- per annum, including ₹5,000/- as maintenance allowance and up to ₹20,000/- as course fees.
Full course fees are reimbursed for the listed institutions.
The annual family income of the applicant must not exceed ₹2,50,000/- from all sources.
Scholarships are awarded directly to the student’s bank account.
Funds are transferred via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to the student's bank account.
Disbursed annually upon approval of the application.
Applicable only for the duration of the course, subject to yearly renewal based on academic performance.
eligibility: The applicant should belong to a notified minority community (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, or Parsi).
The applicant must be a resident of Haryana, irrespective of the place of study.
The applicant should have secured admission to a professional or technical course in a recognized institution.
The applicant should have scored at least 50% marks in the previous qualifying examination.
The annual family income should not exceed ₹2,50,000/- from all sources.
30% of scholarships are reserved for girl students, transferable to male students if no eligible girl candidates are available.

online: Step 1:
Keep ready the soft copies of the required documents.
Go to
the National Scholarship Portal (NSP)
. and click “Apply Now”.
Guidelines for
Registration
will appear. Scroll to the bottom.
Read carefully the undertaking. Accept the Terms. Click “Continue”.
Step 2:
A Registration Form will appear. (The fields marked as * are compulsory.)
Fill in the details and click “Register”.
Your Application ID and password will be displayed.
The same will also be sent as an SMS to your registered mobile number.
Step 3:
Login with OTR
Click on “Login to Apply”. Enter your Application ID and password.
Type the Captcha and click “Login”.
On the next screen, provide the OTP received on your registered mobile number. You will be directed to the Password Reset screen.
Create a new password and confirm.
Click “Submit”. You will be directed to the “Applicant’s Dashboard”.
Step 4:
On the left pane, click “Application Form." The fields marked as * are compulsory. Fill in the details and upload the documents.
You can either click on “Save as Draft” to complete the application later.
Else, click “Final Submit” to submit the application.

documents_required: 3. Self-Declaration of Income Certificate by the Student.
4. Self-Declaration of Community by the Student.
For Fresh Applicants: Previous Academic Year Marksheet.
For Renewal Applicants: Previous Year Marksheet.
6. Fee Receipt for the Current Academic Year.
7. Proof of Bank Account in the Name of the Student.

faqs.question: What is the Merit Cum Means Based Scholarship scheme, and who implements it?
faqs.answer: The Merit Cum Means Based Scholarship scheme, launched by the Ministry of Minority Affairs, Government of India, provides financial assistance to students from minority communities for professional and technical courses. It is implemented through State Governments/UT Administrations.

faqs.question: What are the benefits provided under this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides up to ₹30,000 annually, including ₹10,000 as a maintenance allowance for hostellers and ₹20,000 towards course fees or the actual fee, whichever is lower. Day scholars receive ₹25,000 annually.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: The applicant must belong to a minority community, have at least 50% marks in the previous examination, and secure admission in professional or technical courses. The annual family income should not exceed ₹2.50 lakh.

faqs.question: Can a student receiving another scholarship apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, students already availing themselves of other scholarships or stipends are not eligible for the Merit Cum Means Based Scholarship scheme.

faqs.question: What documents are required for applying under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Mandatory documents include a student photo, income certificate, community declaration, previous academic marksheets, fee receipt, Aadhaar card (optional), and proof of bank account in the student’s name.

faqs.question: How can I apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Applications must be submitted online through the National Scholarship Portal at www.scholarships.gov.in. Students should ensure they meet the eligibility criteria before applying.

faqs.question: What happens if a student discontinues the course after receiving the scholarship?
faqs.answer: If a student discontinues their course, they must refund the scholarship amount. Additionally, students providing false information will be blacklisted from future scholarships.

faqs.question: What is the reservation policy for girl students under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Thirty percent of the scholarships are reserved for girl students in each minority community within a State/UT. If not utilized, this quota may be transferred to eligible male students.

faqs.question: How are students selected if they do not qualify through a competitive exam?
faqs.answer: Students without a competitive exam rank must have at least 50% marks in their last qualifying examination. Selection is based strictly on merit.

faqs.question: What is the grievance redressal mechanism for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants can address grievances through the National Scholarship Portal or the helpline provided on the website. State-level grievance officers also assist in resolving issues.

faqs.question: When are the application opening and closing dates?
faqs.answer: The application period typically opens in the third quarter of the year. Specific dates are announced on the National Scholarship Portal and widely advertised by State Governments.
